name: Neck Bridge (Wrestler's Bridge)
resistance_profile: Supports bodyweight through the cervical spine; primarily an isometric and rocking hold for the neck extensors and stabilizers with fairly constant load.
cues: Build up gradually and keep the hands ready to share the load
Small, controlled range and never jerk or force it
Brace the whole body to protect the neck
execution_steps: Lie on your back with knees bent and feet flat, close to your hips.
Place your hands beside your head or on your chest and drive your hips up into a bridge.
Shift weight onto the crown and back of your head so the neck supports the load.
Gently rock a few inches forward and back, controlling the motion with the neck.
Keep the hands ready to assist and never force range, then lower with control to finish.
good_when: You are an experienced wrestler or grappler needing robust neck strength and endurance
You want a bodyweight, equipment-free way to load the neck
bad_when: You are a beginner or have any neck or cervical spine issues
You cannot keep the neck pain-free and controlled, given the high injury risk if rushed
common_errors: Loading the neck aggressively before it is conditioned
Rocking too far or too fast
progressions: Add a slightly larger controlled front-to-back range
regressions: Keep the hands firmly planted to offload the neck
Perform isometric holds only with minimal range
